Comic science fiction comedy science fiction subgenre soft science fiction science fantasy that exploits science-fiction (SF) genre's conventions comedic effect.
Comic science fiction often mocks satirizes standard conventions such alien invasion Earth, interstellar travel, futuristic technology.
also satirize criticize present-day society.
early example Pete Manx series Henry Kuttner Arthur Barnes (sometimes writing together sometimes separately, under house pen-name Kelvin Kent).

later series cemented Kuttner's reputation most popular early writers comic science fiction: Gallegher series (about drunken inventor narcissistic robot) Hogben series (about family mutant hillbillies).
former appeared Astounding Science Fiction 1943 1948 collected hardcover Robots Have Tails (Gnome, 1952), latter appeared Thrilling Wonder Stories late 1940s.
1950s comedy became more common science fiction.

Kornbluth, Frederik Pohl, Robert Sheckley.
Hitchhiker's Guide Galaxy comic science-fiction series written Douglas Adams.
Originally radio comedy broadcast Radio 1978, later morphed into other formats, including stage shows, novels, comic books, 1981 series, 1984 computer game, 2005 feature film.
prominent series British popular culture, Hitchhiker's Guide Galaxy become international multi-media phenomenon; novels most widely distributed, having been translated into more than languages 2005.
Terry Pratchett's 1981 novel Strata also exemplifies comic science fiction genre.
